navigation_overview: Parabank is a sample banking application. When visitors arrive, they see the sign-in page, which offers an Email / Username and Password form, a Register link for new customers, and a Forgot Password? link for credential recovery. After a successful log-in, users reach their account dashboard displaying every account and balance. The left-hand navigation menu now contains the following options: ① Accounts Overview (default dashboard view), ② Open New Account, ③ Transfer Funds, ④ Bill Pay, ⑤ Request Loan, ⑥ Update Contact Info, ⑦ Manage Cards, ⑧ Investments, ⑨ Account Statements, ⑩ Security Settings, ⑪ Support Center, and ⑫ Log Out.

module_summaries.summary:
Line 1: Users can sign in by entering Email/Username and Password or use the Forgot Password link to recover access.
Line 2: Shows the login form fields, password/email validation rules and authentication result messages (e.g., success or inline errors).

Line 1: Users can create a new account by completing the registration form and submitting personal, contact, SSN and credential information.
Line 2: Shows input fields for name, address, state/ZIP, phone, SSN, username (email), password/confirmation and field-level validation/formatting errors.

Line 1: Users can view their accounts list and review balances and totals; account numbers are displayed as masked clickable links (link not implemented yet).
Line 2: Shows a welcome message and a table of accounts with masked Account Number, Account Type, Current Balance, Account Status, Open Date and a footer total balance.

Line 1: Users can open a new Checking or Savings account by selecting an account type, entering an initial deposit and choosing a funding source.
Line 2: Shows account-type selection cards with features and minimum deposits, Initial Deposit field, Funding Source Account dropdown, real-time validation and error messages.

Line 1: Users can initiate transfers between their ParaBank accounts or to external accounts by selecting transfer type, source, destination and amount.
Line 2: Shows transfer type radio buttons, Transfer Amount, Source Account dropdown (Checking/Savings), destination options or external account/confirm fields and validation messages.

Line 1: Users can submit bill payments by entering payee contact and account details, payment amount and selecting a source account to pay from.
Line 2: Shows payee name/address/phone, payee account and confirm fields, Payment Amount, Source Account dropdown, and success reference or inline error messages.

Line 1: Users can apply for Personal, Auto, or Home loans by selecting a loan type, entering loan amount and down payment and choosing a collateral account.
Line 2: Shows loan-type cards with interest rates and allowed ranges, Loan Amount and Down Payment fields, Collateral Account dropdown, and approval/validation results.

Line 1: Users can edit and submit their contact and address details to update their customer profile.
Line 2: Shows pre-filled fields for First/Last Name, Street Address, City, State, ZIP Code and Phone Number with validation feedback and success/failure messages.

Line 1: Users can request a new debit or credit card linked to an account and manage existing cards (set spending limits, travel notices, change status).
Line 2: Shows a card request form (Card Type, Account to Link, Shipping Address) and card controls (select existing card, new spending limit, travel notice, card status) with submission confirmations.

Line 1: Users can view their investment portfolio snapshot and place buy or sell fund trades by specifying action, fund symbol, quantity and account.
Line 2: Shows current fund holdings, market value, unrealised gain/loss and a trade form (Action, Fund Symbol autocomplete, Quantity, Funding/Destination Account) with trade validation and status.

Line 1: Users can generate account statements for a selected period or custom date range and set e-statement (paperless) preferences.
Line 2: Shows statement period selection or date range, Account dropdown, Generate Statement button and e-statement preference checkbox with Email field and generation status messages.

Line 1: Users can change their password by entering Current Password and a New Password with confirmation and submitting the change.
Line 2: Shows Current/New/Confirm New Password fields inside a collapsible panel with strong-password validation, matching checks and success/error feedback.

Line 1: Users can send secure messages (subject, category, rich-text body, optional attachment) or schedule a callback by specifying reason, preferred date/time and phone.
Line 2: Shows secure message fields (Subject, Category, Message Body, Attachment) and callback fields (Reason, Preferred Date, Time Window, Phone) with ticket/confirmation or inline errors.

test_cases.title: Login with valid email and password
test_cases.preconditions: A registered user exists with the provided email and password
steps: Fill all required fields (Email/Username with a registered email address, Password with a valid password that meets complexity)
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: Flash message "Signed in successfully." appears and user is redirected to the Accounts Overview page.

test_cases.title: Login with valid username and password
test_cases.preconditions: A registered user exists with the provided username and password
steps: Fill all required fields (Email/Username with a registered username, Password with a valid password that meets complexity)
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: Flash message "Signed in successfully." appears and user is redirected to the Accounts Overview page.

test_cases.title: Direct URL access while logged out redirects to login
test_cases.preconditions: User is not logged in
steps: Open a new browser window with no active session (e.g., private/incognito).
In the address bar, enter the URL for the Accounts Overview protected page and press Enter.
Observe the resulting page and navigation behavior.
test_cases.expected_result: The user is redirected to the Login page and the protected Accounts Overview content is not accessible.

test_cases.title: Logout from user menu terminates session and shows login page
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in
steps: Open the user/profile menu in the application UI.
Click the Log Out option from the menu.
Wait for the application to navigate and observe the resulting page.
test_cases.expected_result: The session is terminated and the Login page is displayed; authenticated content is no longer visible.

test_cases.title: Protected routes inaccessible after logout
test_cases.preconditions: User has just logged out after an active session
steps: After logging out and landing on the Login page, enter the URL for a protected page such as Accounts Overview in the address bar and press Enter.
Wait for navigation to complete.
Verify that access to the protected page is not granted and the Login page is displayed instead.
test_cases.expected_result: Attempts to access protected routes after logout redirect to the Login page and protected content is not accessible.

test_cases.title: Browser refresh after logout does not restore authenticated session
test_cases.preconditions: User has just logged out
steps: After logout and arriving at the Login page, refresh the browser using the reload button or Ctrl/Cmd+R.
Wait for the page to finish reloading.
Confirm that no authenticated content reappears and the user remains on the Login page.
test_cases.expected_result: Refreshing the browser after logout does not restore the authenticated session; the Login page remains and protected content is not visible.

test_cases.title: Fail to authenticate with incorrect credentials
test_cases.preconditions: A registered user exists for the provided Email/Username
steps: Fill all required fields (Email/Username with a registered email or username, Password with an incorrect value)
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: Error message "Incorrect email or password. Please try again," is shown, the password field is cleared, and the user is allowed another attempt.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Incorrect email or password. Please try again,

test_cases.title: Page refresh while logged in keeps user logged in
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in on an authenticated page
steps: While on an authenticated page (for example, Accounts Overview), click the browser refresh/reload button or press Ctrl/Cmd+R.
Wait for the page to finish loading.
Check that authenticated content and the left-hand navigation are still visible and no redirect to the Login page occurred.
test_cases.expected_result: After refresh the user remains authenticated and stays on the same authenticated page without being redirected to Login.

test_cases.title: Already-logged-in user navigating to login URL is redirected to authenticated landing page
test_cases.preconditions: User is already logged in
steps: While authenticated, enter the Login page URL in the browser address bar and press Enter.
Wait for navigation to complete.
Verify that the application redirects away from the Login page to the authenticated landing page (for example, Accounts Overview).
test_cases.expected_result: The user is redirected to the authenticated landing page instead of being shown the Login page.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Email/Username is required

test_cases.title: Submit with invalid email format
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields (Email/Username with an invalid email format, Password with a valid password that meets complexity)
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating email must be a valid email format and sign-in is blocked.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Email must be a valid email format

test_cases.title: Submit with password not meeting complexity requirements
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields (Email/Username with a registered email address, Password that does not meet the complexity rules)
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the password does not meet complexity requirements and sign-in is blocked.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Password must be at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character

test_cases.title: Login with password exactly at minimum complexity length
test_cases.preconditions: A registered user exists whose password meets the minimum complexity and is exactly the minimum length
steps: Fill all required fields (Email/Username with a registered email address, Password exactly meeting the minimum complexity and length)
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: Flash message "Signed in successfully." appears and user is redirected to the Accounts Overview page.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Password must be at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character

test_cases.title: Register with valid data (5-digit ZIP)
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values (including a valid 5-digit ZIP Code)
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: "Account created successfully — please sign in" is displayed and the user is redirected to the login page.

test_cases.title: Register with valid data (ZIP+4 format)
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values (including a valid 5+4 ZIP Code)
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: "Account created successfully — please sign in" is displayed and the user is redirected to the login page.

test_cases.title: Phone Number auto-formats on field entry
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all other required fields with valid values
Enter unformatted digits into the Phone Number field and move focus away from the field
test_cases.expected_result: Phone Number is automatically formatted to (123) 456-7890.

test_cases.title: Social Security Number auto-formats on field entry
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all other required fields with valid values
Enter unformatted digits into the Social Security Number field and move focus away from the field
test_cases.expected_result: Social Security Number is automatically formatted to 123-45-6789.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: On submission the system validates field completeness, pattern conformance, and password match

test_cases.title: Submit with invalid Username (non-email format)
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except enter an invalid format in the Username field
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the Username must be a valid email format.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Username must be a valid email format

test_cases.title: Submit with invalid Phone Number format
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except enter an invalid value in the Phone Number field
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the Phone Number must follow the required format.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Phone Number must follow the format (123) 456-7890 with automatic formatting

test_cases.title: Submit with invalid ZIP Code format
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except enter an invalid value in the ZIP Code field
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the ZIP Code must be in an accepted 5-digit or 5+4 format.
test_cases.spec_evidence: ZIP Code must be 5 digits or 5+4 format (12345 or 12345-6789)

test_cases.title: Submit with invalid Social Security Number format
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except enter an invalid value in the Social Security Number field
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the Social Security Number must follow the required format.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Social Security Number must follow the format 123-45-6789 with automatic formatting

test_cases.title: Submit with short Password (below minimum length)
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except enter a Password shorter than the minimum length and set Confirm Password to match that short password
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the Password does not meet the minimum length requirement.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Password must be at least 8 characters

test_cases.title: Submit with Confirm Password mismatch
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values but enter a different value in Confirm Password than in Password
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating Confirm Password must match the Password field.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Confirm Password must match the Password field

test_cases.title: Register using a Password exactly at the minimum length
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values, set Password to a value that is exactly the minimum allowed length and set Confirm Password to match
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: "Account created successfully — please sign in" is displayed and the user is redirected to the login page.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Password must be at least 8 characters

test_cases.title: Dashboard displays welcome message with user's name
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview dashboard is open.
steps: Locate the welcome message area on the Accounts Overview page
Verify the welcome message is visible and contains the logged-in user's name
test_cases.expected_result: Welcome message is displayed and includes the user's name.

test_cases.title: Accounts table displays expected columns in each row
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview dashboard is open.
steps: Inspect each row of the accounts table
Verify Account Number element is present, Account Type is displayed, Current Balance is displayed, Account Status shows an 'Active' badge, and Open Date is displayed for each row
test_cases.expected_result: Each table row shows Account Number, Account Type, Current Balance, Account Status (Active badge), and Open Date.

test_cases.title: Rows are ordered by account creation date (earliest first)
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview dashboard is open.
steps: Collect the Open Date value from every row in the accounts table in their displayed order
Verify the collected Open Date sequence is ordered from earliest to latest
test_cases.expected_result: Rows are ordered by account creation date with the earliest account first.

test_cases.title: Table footer displays total balance equal to sum of row balances
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview dashboard is open.
steps: Read the Current Balance values from all rows and compute their numeric sum
Read the total balance value shown in the table footer and compare it to the computed sum
test_cases.expected_result: The table footer displays a total balance equal to the sum of all Current Balance values.

test_cases.title: Account numbers are masked showing only last four digits
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview dashboard is open.
steps: For each row, read the Account Number text as displayed in the table
Verify the displayed Account Number is masked such that only the last four digits are visible and preceding digits are masked
test_cases.expected_result: Account numbers are masked in the table and only the last four digits are visible.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Account numbers are masked for security (showing only last 4 digits as ****5001)

test_cases.title: Clicking Account Number does not navigate (clickable but not implemented yet)
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview dashboard is open.
steps: Click the Account Number element in a table row
Observe the page and verify that no account detail page opens and the dashboard remains displayed
test_cases.expected_result: Account Number appears as a clickable element but clicking it does not navigate or open a detail view.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Account Number (clickable but not implemented yet)

test_cases.title: Open a Checking account successfully
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Open New Account form.
steps: Select the Checking account type card
Fill all required fields (Initial Deposit Amount: an amount meeting the minimum for the selected account type, Funding Source Account: an account with sufficient balance)
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Shows "Account opened successfully!" and redirects to accounts overview where the new account appears in the accounts listing.

test_cases.title: Open a Savings account successfully
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Open New Account form.
steps: Select the Savings account type card
Fill all required fields (Initial Deposit Amount: an amount meeting the minimum for the selected account type, Funding Source Account: an account with sufficient balance)
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Shows "Account opened successfully!" and redirects to accounts overview where the new account appears in the accounts listing.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Open New Account form.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Validation error shown if account type is not selected

test_cases.title: Attempt to open account without selecting account type
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Open New Account form.
steps: Fill all other required fields, leave Account type unselected (Initial Deposit Amount: a value meeting the minimum, Funding Source Account: an account with sufficient balance)
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the account type must be selected.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Account type must be selected

test_cases.title: Attempt to open account with non-numeric initial deposit
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Open New Account form.
steps: Select an account type
Fill all other required fields but enter a non-numeric value in Initial Deposit Amount (Funding Source Account: an account with sufficient balance)
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown indicating the Initial Deposit Amount must be numeric.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Initial Deposit Amount must be numeric

test_cases.title: Reject Checking account when initial deposit is below Checking minimum
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Open New Account form.
steps: Select the Checking account type card
Fill all other required fields and enter an Initial Deposit Amount below the required minimum (Funding Source Account: an account with sufficient balance)
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown indicating the Initial Deposit Amount is below the minimum for Checking.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Initial Deposit Amount must meet minimums: $25 for Checking

test_cases.title: Reject Savings account when initial deposit is below Savings minimum
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Open New Account form.
steps: Select the Savings account type card
Fill all other required fields and enter an Initial Deposit Amount below the required minimum (Funding Source Account: an account with sufficient balance)
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown indicating the Initial Deposit Amount is below the minimum for Savings.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Initial Deposit Amount must meet minimums: $100 for Savings

test_cases.title: Reject opening when funding source account lacks sufficient balance
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Open New Account form.
steps: Select an account type and enter an Initial Deposit Amount that meets the account minimum
Select a Funding Source Account that does not have enough balance
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown indicating the funding source account does not have sufficient balance.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Funding Source Account must have sufficient balance

test_cases.title: Accept initial deposit exactly equal to Checking minimum
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Open New Account form.
steps: Select the Checking account type card
Fill all required fields (Initial Deposit Amount: an amount equal to the Checking minimum, Funding Source Account: an account with sufficient balance)
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Shows "Account opened successfully!" and redirects to accounts overview where the new Checking account appears.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Initial Deposit Amount must meet minimums: $25 for Checking

test_cases.title: Accept initial deposit exactly equal to Savings minimum
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Open New Account form.
steps: Select the Savings account type card
Fill all required fields (Initial Deposit Amount: an amount equal to the Savings minimum, Funding Source Account: an account with sufficient balance)
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Shows "Account opened successfully!" and redirects to accounts overview where the new Savings account appears.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Initial Deposit Amount must meet minimums: $100 for Savings

test_cases.title: Real-time validation displays error for invalid deposit input
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Open New Account form.
steps: Select an account type
Enter an invalid value into the Initial Deposit Amount field without submitting
Observe inline validation message for the Initial Deposit Amount field
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation message appears in real time for the invalid Initial Deposit Amount input.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Displays real-time validation errors

test_cases.title: Successful internal transfer between own accounts
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Transfer Funds page and has at least two own accounts with sufficient balance.
steps: Fill all required fields (Source Account: select one own Checking or Savings account, Destination: select another own account under My ParaBank Account, Transfer Amount: enter a valid amount)
Click "Transfer"
test_cases.expected_result: Success message "Transfer completed successfully." is shown and a transaction ID is displayed.

test_cases.title: Successful external transfer with matching account confirmation
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Transfer Funds page and has an account with sufficient balance.
steps: Select External Account as destination; fill all required fields (Source Account: select Checking or Savings, External Account Number: enter account number, Confirm Account Number: enter the same account number, Transfer Amount: enter a valid amount)
Click "Transfer"
test_cases.expected_result: Success message "Transfer completed successfully." is shown and a transaction ID is displayed.

test_cases.title: Transfer fails when source account has insufficient funds
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Transfer Funds page and the selected source account has insufficient balance.
steps: Fill all required fields with a Transfer Amount greater than the source account balance
Click "Transfer"
test_cases.expected_result: Contextual error indicating insufficient funds is displayed and the transfer is not completed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system validates sufficient funds

test_cases.title: External transfer fails when account numbers do not match
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Transfer Funds page.
steps: Select External Account as destination; fill required fields with different values in External Account Number and Confirm Account Number
Click "Transfer"
test_cases.expected_result: Error "Account numbers do not match." is displayed and the transfer is blocked.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Account numbers do not match.

test_cases.title: Destination options update based on transfer type (internal vs external)
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Transfer Funds page.
steps: Select Internal transfer type and observe Destination options
Select External transfer type and observe that External Account Number and Confirm Account Number fields appear
test_cases.expected_result: Destination options change appropriately based on selected transfer type.

test_cases.title: Source Account dropdown shows only Checking and Savings accounts
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Transfer Funds page.
steps: Open the Source Account dropdown and inspect the listed account types
test_cases.expected_result: The Source Account dropdown lists only Checking and Savings accounts.

test_cases.title: Submit transfer with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Transfer Funds page.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Transfer"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system validates transfer amount

test_cases.title: External transfer with confirm account number empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Transfer Funds page.
steps: Select External Account as destination; fill all other required fields and leave Confirm Account Number empty
Click "Transfer"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating account confirmation is required or that account numbers must match.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system validates account number matching for external transfers

test_cases.title: Reject transfer when transfer amount is not a valid number
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Transfer Funds page.
steps: Fill all required fields using a non-numeric or improperly formatted value in the Transfer Amount field
Click "Transfer"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the transfer amount is invalid and the transfer is not submitted.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system validates transfer amount

test_cases.title: Submit a payment with valid details
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and on the Payments form.
steps: Fill all required fields (Payee Name, Street Address, City, State, ZIP Code, Phone Number, Payee Account Number, Confirm Account Number, Payment Amount)
Select the Source Account from the dropdown
Click "Pay"
test_cases.expected_result: Payment submitted successfully with a reference code and account balances updated.

test_cases.title: Correct validation error and resubmit the payment
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and on the Payments form.
steps: Fill fields to produce a validation error and click "Pay"
Correct the invalid fields (for example, make Payee Account Number and Confirm Account Number match) and ensure Payment Amount is within available funds
Click "Pay"
test_cases.expected_result: Payment submitted successfully with a reference code and account balances updated.

test_cases.title: Submit with mismatched payee account numbers
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and on the Payments form.
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values but enter different values for Payee Account Number and Confirm Account Number
Select the Source Account from the dropdown
Click "Pay"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error 'Account numbers do not match' is displayed and the form remains editable.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Payee Account Number and Confirm Account Number must match

test_cases.title: Submit a payment exceeding available funds
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and on the Payments form.
steps: Fill all required fields with a Payment Amount greater than the selected Source Account's available balance
Select the Source Account from the dropdown
Click "Pay"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error 'Insufficient funds' is displayed and the form remains editable.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Displays 'Insufficient funds' inline

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and on the Payments form.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Pay"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Displays validation errors inline

test_cases.title: Form remains editable after a validation error
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and on the Payments form.
steps: Fill fields so a validation error will occur (for example, mismatched Payee Account Number and Confirm Account Number)
Click "Pay"
Attempt to edit any previously-entered field
test_cases.expected_result: Form fields remain editable after the validation error is shown.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Form remains editable after errors

test_cases.title: Request Personal loan with valid inputs and approved credit
test_cases.preconditions: Credit engine returns approval and collateral account has sufficient funds.
steps: Select the Personal loan card

Fill all required fields (Loan Amount within Personal allowed range, Down Payment satisfying minimum percentage and less than Loan Amount, select Collateral Account with sufficient funds, choose Interest Rate if required)
Click "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Displays "Loan approved and created successfully!" and shows the newly created loan account details.

test_cases.title: Request Auto loan with valid inputs and approved credit
test_cases.preconditions: Credit engine returns approval and collateral account has sufficient funds.
steps: Select the Auto loan card

Fill all required fields (Loan Amount within Auto allowed range, Down Payment satisfying minimum percentage and less than Loan Amount, select Collateral Account with sufficient funds, choose Interest Rate if required)
Click "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Displays "Loan approved and created successfully!" and shows the newly created loan account details.

test_cases.title: Request Home loan with valid inputs and approved credit
test_cases.preconditions: Credit engine returns approval and collateral account has sufficient funds.
steps: Select the Home loan card

Fill all required fields (Loan Amount within Home allowed range, Down Payment satisfying minimum percentage and less than Loan Amount, select Collateral Account with sufficient funds, choose Interest Rate if required)
Click "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Displays "Loan approved and created successfully!" and shows the newly created loan account details.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Validation errors shown for amount ranges

test_cases.title: Submit when Down Payment is not less than Loan Amount
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Select any loan type card
Fill all required fields, set Down Payment equal to or greater than the Loan Amount, select Collateral Account
Click "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown indicating down payment must be less than the loan amount.
test_cases.spec_evidence: down payment < loan amount

test_cases.title: Submit when Down Payment is below minimum required percentage
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Select any loan type card
Fill all required fields with a Down Payment below the minimum required percentage and valid Loan Amount and Collateral selection
Click "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown indicating the minimum down payment requirement is not met.
test_cases.spec_evidence: 10% minimum down payment requirement

test_cases.title: Submit when selected Collateral Account has insufficient funds
test_cases.preconditions: Collateral account balance is insufficient relative to requested loan and collateral rules.
steps: Select any loan type card
Fill all required fields (valid Loan Amount and Down Payment), select a Collateral Account with insufficient funds
Click "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown indicating collateral funds are insufficient.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Validation errors shown when collateral funds are insufficient

test_cases.title: Submit when Collateral value does not meet 20% requirement
test_cases.preconditions: Collateral account exists but its value is below the required percentage of the loan.
steps: Select any loan type card
Fill all required fields (valid Loan Amount and Down Payment), select a Collateral Account whose value results in less than the required collateral percentage
Click "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown indicating the collateral value requirement is not met.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Validation errors shown when 20% collateral value requirement is not met

test_cases.title: Loan request denied by credit engine showing credit-related reason
test_cases.preconditions: Credit engine returns denial for this request.
steps: Select any loan type card
Fill all required fields with valid Loan Amount, Down Payment, and Collateral selection
Click "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Loan is denied and a specific denial reason is displayed, for example "Insufficient credit history".
test_cases.spec_evidence: Insufficient credit history

test_cases.title: Approved loan does not debit collateral account balance in mock system
test_cases.preconditions: Credit engine returns approval and collateral account has sufficient funds.
steps: Select any loan type card
Fill all required fields with valid Loan Amount, Down Payment, and select Collateral Account with a known balance
Click "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Loan is approved and created but the collateral account balance remains unchanged in the UI.
test_cases.spec_evidence: In this mock system, no actual balance debits occur

test_cases.title: Update profile with valid contact information
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Update Profile page is open.
steps: Fill all required fields (First Name, Last Name, Street Address, City, State, ZIP Code, Phone Number) with valid values
Click "Update Profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Profile updated successfully. and page displays the refreshed profile data.

test_cases.title: Updated profile data persists and is displayed after save
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Update Profile page is open.
steps: Fill all required fields (First Name, Last Name, Street Address, City, State, ZIP Code, Phone Number) with new valid values
Click "Update Profile"
Verify the displayed profile fields show the new values
test_cases.expected_result: Detail page reflects the updated values and shows "Profile updated successfully."

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Update Profile page is open.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Update Profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Each entry is validated for completeness

test_cases.title: Submit with invalid ZIP Code format
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Update Profile page is open.
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except enter an invalid value into the ZIP Code field
Click "Update Profile"
test_cases.expected_result: ZIP Code field is highlighted and an inline error banner is displayed indicating format is invalid.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Each entry is validated for format

test_cases.title: Submit with invalid Phone Number format
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Update Profile page is open.
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except enter an invalid value into the Phone Number field
Click "Update Profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Phone Number field is highlighted and an inline error banner is displayed indicating format is invalid.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Each entry is validated for format

test_cases.title: Submit with multiple invalid fields shows highlighted fields and inline error banner
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Update Profile page is open.
steps: Fill all required fields with values that cause at least two format/validation failures
Click "Update Profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Invalid fields are highlighted and an inline error banner is displayed describing the validation errors.
test_cases.spec_evidence: display an inline error banner

test_cases.title: Request a debit card with valid account and complete shipping address
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Manage Cards - Request Card page.
steps: Select Card Type as Debit and select an account in good standing in the Account to Link control
Fill Shipping Address with a complete address (all required address fields)
Click "Request Card"
test_cases.expected_result: A card-request ticket is created and the page shows "Card request submitted successfully." with a tracking ID.

test_cases.title: Request a credit card with valid account and complete shipping address
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Manage Cards - Request Card page.
steps: Select Card Type as Credit and select an account in good standing in the Account to Link control
Fill Shipping Address with a complete address (all required address fields)
Click "Request Card"
test_cases.expected_result: A card-request ticket is created and the page shows "Card request submitted successfully." with a tracking ID.

test_cases.title: Update card controls with valid limit, travel notice, and status change
test_cases.preconditions: An existing card is available in the Select Existing Card dropdown.
steps: Select an existing card from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Fill all required fields (New Spending Limit with a valid numeric amount, Travel Notice dates and destinations, Card Status)
Click "Update Controls"
test_cases.expected_result: Confirmation message "Card controls updated successfully." is displayed and the form reflects the updated controls.

test_cases.title: Update card controls without travel notice (travel notice optional)
test_cases.preconditions: An existing card is available in the Select Existing Card dropdown.
steps: Select an existing card from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Fill all required fields (New Spending Limit with a valid numeric amount, leave Travel Notice empty, Card Status)
Click "Update Controls"
test_cases.expected_result: Confirmation message "Card controls updated successfully." is displayed and the form reflects the updated controls.

test_cases.title: Change card status to Frozen with valid inputs
test_cases.preconditions: An existing card currently in Active status is available in the Select Existing Card dropdown.
steps: Select the Active card from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Fill all required fields (New Spending Limit with a valid numeric amount, Card Status set to Frozen)
Click "Update Controls"
test_cases.expected_result: Confirmation message "Card controls updated successfully." is displayed and the card status is updated to Frozen.

test_cases.title: Submit card request when selected account's standing is not verified
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Manage Cards - Request Card page.
steps: Select Card Type and select an account known to have unverified or ineligible standing in the Account to Link control
Fill Shipping Address with a complete address (all required address fields)
Click "Request Card"
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is rejected and an error is shown indicating the selected account's standing prevents card issuance.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Selected account’s standing must be verified

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Manage Cards - Request Card page.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Request Card"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors are shown for required fields and the card request is not submitted.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Shipping Address must be complete

test_cases.title: Submit with incomplete shipping address
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Manage Cards - Request Card page.
steps: Select a valid Card Type and select an account in good standing in the Account to Link control
Fill Shipping Address with incomplete/missing address fields
Click "Request Card"
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is blocked and a validation error indicates the shipping address must be completed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Shipping Address must be complete

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Update Controls"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Validation failures (for example, a limit above policy) appear inline

test_cases.title: Reject update when new spending limit is above policy
test_cases.preconditions: An existing card is available in the Select Existing Card dropdown.
steps: Select an existing card from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Fill all required fields (New Spending Limit with an amount above policy, Card Status)
Click "Update Controls"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicates the spending limit exceeds policy and the form remains editable.
test_cases.spec_evidence: A limit above policy is a validation failure

test_cases.title: Reject update when travel notice date range is invalid
test_cases.preconditions: An existing card is available in the Select Existing Card dropdown.
steps: Select an existing card from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Fill all required fields (Travel Notice with an end date earlier than the start date, New Spending Limit with a valid numeric amount, Card Status)
Click "Update Controls"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicates an invalid travel notice date range and the form remains editable.
test_cases.spec_evidence: System validates date ranges

test_cases.title: Reject disallowed card-status transition
test_cases.preconditions: An existing card is in a status that prevents the requested transition and is available in the Select Existing Card dropdown.
steps: Select the card whose current status prevents the requested transition from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Fill all required fields (Card Status set to the disallowed target status, New Spending Limit with a valid numeric amount)
Click "Update Controls"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicates the status transition is not allowed and the form remains editable.
test_cases.spec_evidence: System validates allowable card-status transitions

test_cases.title: Accept update when new spending limit equals policy maximum
test_cases.preconditions: An existing card is available in the Select Existing Card dropdown and the policy maximum is known.
steps: Select an existing card from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Fill all required fields (New Spending Limit set to the policy maximum allowed amount, Card Status)
Click "Update Controls"
test_cases.expected_result: Confirmation message "Card controls updated successfully." is displayed and the spending limit is updated to the policy maximum.
test_cases.spec_evidence: A limit above policy is a validation failure

test_cases.title: Execute a Buy trade successfully updates holdings
test_cases.workflow: Execute trade using the trade funds form
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the trade funds form is visible; funding account has sufficient buying power.
steps: Fill all required fields (Action = Buy, Fund Symbol with a valid existing symbol, Quantity with a positive number, Funding Account selected)
Click "Execute Trade"
test_cases.expected_result: Same-day trade executes; "Trade executed successfully." with an order ID is displayed and holdings update in the portfolio snapshot.

test_cases.title: Execute a Sell trade successfully updates holdings
test_cases.workflow: Execute trade using the trade funds form
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the trade funds form is visible; user holds at least the specified quantity of the fund.
steps: Fill all required fields (Action = Sell, Fund Symbol with an owned fund, Quantity less than or equal to held shares, Destination Account selected)
Click "Execute Trade"
test_cases.expected_result: Same-day trade executes; "Trade executed successfully." with an order ID is displayed and holdings are reduced accordingly in the portfolio snapshot.

test_cases.title: Create recurring investment plan with Weekly frequency (success)
test_cases.workflow: Create plan using the recurring investment plan form
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the recurring investment plan form.
steps: Fill all required fields (Fund Symbol, Contribution Amount, Frequency set to Weekly, Start Date in the future, Funding Account with adequate balance)
Click "Create Plan"
test_cases.expected_result: "Plan created successfully." is displayed and the new recurring schedule appears in the user's investment plans listing.

test_cases.title: Create recurring investment plan with Monthly frequency (success)
test_cases.workflow: Create plan using the recurring investment plan form
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the recurring investment plan form.
steps: Fill all required fields (Fund Symbol, Contribution Amount, Frequency set to Monthly, Start Date in the future, Funding Account with adequate balance)
Click "Create Plan"
test_cases.expected_result: "Plan created successfully." is displayed and the plan is stored.

test_cases.title: Buy trade fails when funding account lacks sufficient buying power
test_cases.workflow: Execute trade using the trade funds form
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the trade funds form is visible; funding account has insufficient buying power.
steps: Fill all required fields (Action = Buy, Fund Symbol valid, Quantity that exceeds buying power, Funding Account selected)
Click "Execute Trade"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicates insufficient buying power and trade is not executed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: the customer has sufficient buying power or share balance

test_cases.title: Sell trade fails when share balance is insufficient
test_cases.workflow: Execute trade using the trade funds form
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the trade funds form is visible; user holds fewer shares than the requested quantity.
steps: Fill all required fields (Action = Sell, Fund Symbol of held fund, Quantity greater than held shares, Destination Account selected)
Click "Execute Trade"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicates insufficient share balance and trade is not executed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: the customer has sufficient buying power or share balance

test_cases.title: Reject plan when Start Date is in the past
test_cases.workflow: Create plan using the recurring investment plan form
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the recurring investment plan form.
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except set Start Date to a past date
Click "Create Plan"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error is shown indicating the start date must be in the future and the Start Date field is highlighted.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Start date must be in the future

test_cases.title: Reject plan when funding account has inadequate balance
test_cases.workflow: Create plan using the recurring investment plan form
test_cases.preconditions: A funding account with insufficient balance exists and is selectable in the form.
steps: Fill all required fields selecting the funding account with insufficient balance and other fields valid
Click "Create Plan"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error is shown indicating the funding account does not have adequate balance and the Funding Account field is highlighted.
test_cases.spec_evidence: the funding account has adequate balance

test_cases.title: Portfolio snapshot is read-only and shows holdings and values
test_cases.workflow: Execute trade using the trade funds form
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the trade funds form is visible.
steps: View the portfolio snapshot panel on the trade funds form page
test_cases.expected_result: Portfolio snapshot is read-only and displays current fund holdings, market value, and unrealised gain or loss.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.workflow: Execute trade using the trade funds form
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the trade funds form is visible.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Execute Trade"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Validation failures for the trade appear inline

test_cases.title: Submit trade with a non-existent fund symbol
test_cases.workflow: Execute trade using the trade funds form
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the trade funds form is visible.
steps: Fill all required fields (Action, Fund Symbol with a non-existent symbol, Quantity with a positive number, Funding/Destination Account selected)
Click "Execute Trade"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicates the fund symbol does not exist and trade is not executed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system checks that the symbol exists

test_cases.title: Submit trade with quantity set to zero
test_cases.workflow: Execute trade using the trade funds form
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the trade funds form is visible.
steps: Fill all required fields (Action selected, Fund Symbol valid, Quantity set to zero, Funding/Destination Account selected)
Click "Execute Trade"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicates quantity must be greater than zero and trade is not executed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: quantity is greater than zero

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.workflow: Create plan using the recurring investment plan form
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the recurring investment plan form.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Create Plan"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown and problematic fields are highlighted.
test_cases.spec_evidence: highlight the problematic fields

test_cases.title: Reject plan when contribution amount is below the minimum
test_cases.workflow: Create plan using the recurring investment plan form
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the recurring investment plan form.
steps: Fill all required fields with Contribution Amount below the allowed minimum and other fields valid
Click "Create Plan"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error is shown indicating the contribution does not meet the minimum and the Contribution Amount field is highlighted.
test_cases.spec_evidence: the contribution meets the minimum

test_cases.title: Sell trade succeeds when quantity equals available share balance (boundary)
test_cases.workflow: Execute trade using the trade funds form
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the trade funds form is visible; user holds exactly the specified quantity of the fund.
steps: Fill all required fields (Action = Sell, Fund Symbol of held fund, Quantity equal to held shares, Destination Account selected)
Click "Execute Trade"
test_cases.expected_result: Same-day trade executes; "Trade executed successfully." with an order ID is displayed and holdings are updated to reflect the sale.
test_cases.spec_evidence: the customer has sufficient buying power or share balance

test_cases.title: Reject plan when Start Date equals today's date (boundary)
test_cases.workflow: Create plan using the recurring investment plan form
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the recurring investment plan form.
steps: Fill all required fields with Start Date set to today's date and other fields valid
Click "Create Plan"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error is shown indicating the start date must be in the future and the Start Date field is highlighted.
test_cases.spec_evidence: the system validates that the start date is in the future

test_cases.title: Generate statement using month-and-year period
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Account Statements page is open.
steps: Select Statement Period as month-and-year and choose a valid month and year; select an Account from the Account dropdown
Click "Generate Statement"
test_cases.expected_result: Statement generated successfully and confirmation or statement view is displayed.

test_cases.title: Generate statement using custom date range
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Account Statements page is open.
steps: Select Statement Period as custom date range and fill valid start and end dates where start is on or before end; select an Account from the Account dropdown
Click "Generate Statement"
test_cases.expected_result: Statement generated successfully and confirmation or statement view is displayed.

test_cases.title: Generated statement lists transactions within the selected period
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Account Statements page is open.
steps: Select a statement period (month-and-year or custom date range) and select an Account
Click "Generate Statement"
Verify the displayed transactions have dates that fall within the selected period
test_cases.expected_result: The system retrieves the relevant transactions and the listed transactions' dates fall inside the chosen period.

test_cases.title: Save paperless preference with valid email
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Account Statements page and Save Preference panel is visible
steps: Check the paperless statements checkbox
Fill the Email Address field with a valid email address
Click the Save Preference button
test_cases.expected_result: e-Statement preference updated.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Account Statements page is open.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Generate Statement"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for the date and/or account fields and statement generation is blocked.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system validates the dates

test_cases.title: Attempt generation with end date before start date
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Account Statements page is open.
steps: Select Statement Period as custom date range and set the end date earlier than the start date; select an Account
Click "Generate Statement"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating invalid date range is shown and statement is not generated.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system validates the dates

test_cases.title: Handle server error when generating statement
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Account Statements page is open.
steps: Fill all required fields with valid period and select an Account
Click "Generate Statement"
test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed indicating the statement could not be generated and advising to try again later.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Unable to generate statement — please try again later.

test_cases.title: Save Preference with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Account Statements page and Save Preference panel is visible
steps: Leave the paperless statements checkbox unchecked and leave the Email Address field empty
Click the Save Preference button
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for required fields and email input is highlighted with guidance.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Failure highlights the email field with guidance

test_cases.title: Save Preference with checkbox checked but Email Address empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Account Statements page and Save Preference panel is visible
steps: Check the paperless statements checkbox and leave the Email Address field empty
Click the Save Preference button
test_cases.expected_result: Email field is highlighted with guidance and preference is not saved.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Failure highlights the email field with guidance

test_cases.title: Save Preference with invalid email format
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Account Statements page and Save Preference panel is visible
steps: Check the paperless statements checkbox
Fill the Email Address field with an invalidly formatted email
Click the Save Preference button
test_cases.expected_result: Email field is highlighted with validation guidance and preference is not saved.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system validates the email address

test_cases.title: Generate statement for period with no transactions
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Account Statements page is open.
steps: Select a statement period (month-and-year or custom date range) that is expected to contain no transactions; select an Account
Click "Generate Statement"
Verify the statement is generated and the transactions list is empty or shows an appropriate no-transactions message
test_cases.expected_result: Statement is generated and the UI indicates no transactions were retrieved for the selected period.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system retrieves the relevant transactions

test_cases.title: Change password with valid current and matching strong new password
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Security Settings > Change Password panel is open.
steps: Fill all required fields (Current Password, New Password, Confirm New Password) with the correct current password and a new strong password that meets policy and matches confirmation
Click "Change Password"
test_cases.expected_result: Password changed successfully and a success confirmation is displayed.

test_cases.title: Change password with incorrect current password
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Security Settings > Change Password panel is open.
steps: Fill all required fields (Current Password with an incorrect value, New Password and Confirm New Password with a matching strong new password)
Click "Change Password"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating current password is incorrect and the password is not changed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system verifies the current password

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Security Settings > Change Password panel is open.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Change Password"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Validation errors highlight the appropriate fields.

test_cases.title: Change password with weak new password that fails strength policy
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Security Settings > Change Password panel is open.
steps: Fill all required fields (Current Password with correct value, New Password and Confirm New Password with a weak password that does not meet strength requirements)
Click "Change Password"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the new password does not meet strength requirements and the password is not changed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system enforces a strong-password policy

test_cases.title: Change password with non-matching New Password and Confirm New Password
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Security Settings > Change Password panel is open.
steps: Fill all required fields (Current Password with correct value, New Password with a valid strong password, Confirm New Password with a different value)
Click "Change Password"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating New Password and Confirm New Password do not match and the password is not changed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system checks that New Password and Confirm New Password match

test_cases.title: Collapse and expand Change Password panel preserves entered values
test_cases.preconditions: User is signed in and the Security Settings page is open with the Change Password panel visible.
steps: Fill some or all fields in the Change Password form (Current Password, New Password, Confirm New Password) without submitting
Collapse the Change Password panel
Expand the Change Password panel
test_cases.expected_result: Previously entered values remain populated in the form fields after collapsing and expanding the panel.

test_cases.title: Send message successfully without attachment
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields (Category, Subject, Message Body)
Click "Send Message"
test_cases.expected_result: Displays "Message sent successfully." and shows a ticket ID

test_cases.title: Send message successfully with a valid attachment
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields (Category, Subject, Message Body) and attach a valid file type
Click "Send Message"
test_cases.expected_result: Displays "Message sent successfully." and shows a ticket ID

test_cases.title: Send message with rich-text formatting in the message body
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields (Category, Subject) and compose Message Body using rich-text formatting
Click "Send Message"
test_cases.expected_result: Displays "Message sent successfully." and shows a ticket ID

test_cases.title: Submit callback request with valid inputs
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and the Request Callback form is open.
steps: Fill all required fields (select Reason for Call, set Preferred Date to a valid date at or after the next business day, select Preferred Time Window, ensure Phone Number contains a valid phone number)
Click "Request Callback"
test_cases.expected_result: Callback request submitted and an email confirmation is sent.

test_cases.title: Submit with Preferred Date before the next business day
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and the Request Callback form is open.
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except set Preferred Date to an ineligible date (not at least the next business day)
Click "Request Callback"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicating the selected date is not allowed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system ensures the date is at least the next business day

test_cases.title: Request Callback form pre-fills phone number and allows edit
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and the Request Callback form is open.
steps: Verify Phone Number field is pre-filled with the user's number and edit it to a different valid phone number
Fill all other required fields (select Reason for Call, set Preferred Date to a valid date at or after the next business day, select Preferred Time Window)
Click "Request Callback"
test_cases.expected_result: Callback request submitted and an email confirmation is sent.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Send Message"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields and inline guidance is displayed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Failures display inline guidance

test_cases.title: Submit with message body empty
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all other required fields (Category, Subject), leave Message Body empty
Click "Send Message"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the message body is required and inline guidance is shown.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Ensures a message body is present

test_cases.title: Submit with subject exceeding allowed length
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with a subject longer than allowed and a valid Message Body
Click "Send Message"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the subject length is invalid and inline guidance is shown.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system checks subject length

test_cases.title: Submit with an invalid attachment type
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields (Category, Subject, Message Body) and attach an unsupported file type
Click "Send Message"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the attachment type is not allowed and inline guidance is shown.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Validates attachment types

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and the Request Callback form is open.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Request Callback"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Validation errors are shown inline

test_cases.title: Submit with invalid phone number format
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and the Request Callback form is open.
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values except set Phone Number to an invalid phone number format
Click "Request Callback"
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error shown for phone number format.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Validates the phone number format

test_cases.title: Submit with Preferred Date equal to the next business day (boundary)
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and the Request Callback form is open.
steps: Fill all required fields (select Reason for Call, set Preferred Date to the next business day, select Preferred Time Window, ensure Phone Number is valid)
Click "Request Callback"
test_cases.expected_result: Callback request submitted and an email confirmation is sent.
test_cases.spec_evidence: The system ensures the date is at least the next business day
